source_point_summary: If I trust the library and later discover corrupted knowledge, trust collapses.
routing_rationale: This is a core owner threshold and also the foundation of the engineering risk model.

source_point_summary: One mistake is enough to put all knowledge coming from the library in question.
routing_rationale: This is the clearest owner statement of global trust poisoning.

source_point_summary: Silent errors are especially dangerous because they are discovered only later.
routing_rationale: The owner answer names the fear; the engineering layer expands the silent-failure family.

source_point_summary: Dangerous handling of knowledge includes wrongful excerpting, bad cutting, and similar corruption.
routing_rationale: A direct content-integrity fear becomes both owner answer material and machine constraints.

source_point_summary: Any architecture built on loose grounds is unacceptable because engines are interlinked.
routing_rationale: Owner fear becomes a structural prevention rule.

source_point_summary: Losing progress or losing the app is a nightmare scenario.
routing_rationale: Direct owner fear mapped into preservation requirements.

source_point_summary: Later realizing the library could have been better would mean years of reduced scholarship.
routing_rationale: This is explicitly owner-facing but also generates the broader scholarship-cap frontier.

source_point_summary: Cost blow-up or extreme slowness would make the library unusable at full scale.
routing_rationale: The owner statement becomes a corpus-scale viability family.

source_point_summary: Extreme granulation can make excerpts unreadable atoms.
routing_rationale: Direct owner fear kept in owner answer and represented concretely in the scenario canon.

source_point_summary: Collected data must be structurally saved because it may later train a local model.
routing_rationale: Direct owner statement becomes both trust scenario and preservation constraint.

source_point_summary: The project must identify and use any tools, libraries, APIs, or models that materially improve possibilities.
routing_rationale: Owner concern is preserved and then expanded into a broader missed-possibility frontier.

source_point_summary: Agents working in a suboptimal environment are themselves a risk.
routing_rationale: Too implementation-facing for the questionnaire answer, but central to prevention.

source_point_summary: Not doing enough research despite having top research models available is dangerous.
routing_rationale: Owner threshold preserved; engineering canon turns it into auditable prevention.

source_point_summary: Agent blind spots and weak collaboration can leave major issues undiscovered.
routing_rationale: Real risk, but better kept outside the owner answer except in generalized wording.

source_point_summary: The library must not merely work; it must avoid capping scholarship.
routing_rationale: This is a defining owner threshold and the root of the scholarship-cap category.

source_point_summary: Prior chat established sensitivity to quote-layer, taxonomy, relation, and omission errors.
routing_rationale: These were not explicitly restated in the draft, so they stay outside the owner answer but are treated as owner-consistent engineering risks.

source_point_summary: Prior chat established that false confidence from polished presentation is dangerous because it accelerates bad study.
routing_rationale: This is derived from the user's insistence on safe study units and distrust of silent failure, not from an explicit draft sentence.

source_point_summary: Model-expanded frontier covers lifecycle, dependency, backup, version drift, evaluation blind spots, and other non-obvious trust destroyers.
routing_rationale: These are intentionally separated from owner truth while still preserved for prevention.
